coverBlurb: Growth & Brains is a performance marketing agency redefining how brands understand and influence customer behaviour by blending neuroscience, behavioural psychology, and data-driven strategy.
articleSubheadline: Andrew Redfern on why understanding how people think is the ultimate competitive advantage in modern marketing
articleBody: Growth & Brains is a performance marketing agency redefining how brands understand and influence customer behaviour. Founded on the principle that marketing should mirror how people actually decide — not just how they browse — the agency blends neuroscience, behavioural psychology, and data-driven strategy to create campaigns that connect on an instinctive level.

Led by a founder with over two decades of experience in digital marketing, published research in brand reputation mining, and an MBA from the University of Liverpool, Growth & Brains operates at the intersection of commercial strategy and human psychology.

Alongside leading the agency, Andrew Redfern also serves as Chairman of the UKH Foundation, a UK-based charity that has contributed over £3.5 million to health-related causes across the North West of England.

A real-world example: Ripe Thinking's Golf Care mobile homepage was underperforming because a key incentive — the free gift — was being overlooked. Traditional optimisation methods like A/B testing or eye-tracking would have taken weeks to fix. Instead, Growth & Brains applied behavioural science to rapidly identify and solve the issue, delivering a 33% uplift in golf insurance conversions.
pullQuote: Growth & Brains stands for a simple but powerful belief: when you understand how people think, marketing stops being guesswork and starts becoming growth science.
pullQuoteAuthor: Andrew Redfern, Founder, Growth and Brains
caseStudyHeadline: How Neuroscience Transformed Mobile Conversions in Insurance Marketing
caseStudyBody: By applying behavioural science and AI to Ripe Thinking's Golf Care homepage, Growth & Brains rapidly identified why a key incentive was being overlooked — and fixed it in a fraction of the time traditional methods would have taken.
